habitat: Coastal areas; mangroves; open woodlands; grasslands; gardens; parks; and wetlands
story: Few birds capture the imagination quite like the Collared Kingfisher (Todiramphus chloris). As a member of the Alcedinidae family, it has carved out a unique niche in Coastal areas; mangroves; open woodlands; grasslands; gardens; parks; and wetlands. Its conservation status is Least Concern (LC), a classification that drives crucial research and protection efforts. The Collared Kingfisher's presence is a vital sign of its ecosystem's health, making its preservation a priority. This poster is a tribute to the Collared Kingfisher, celebrating its role in the natural world and inspiring a deeper appreciation for biodiversity.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
